name: Jordan Taylor
occupation: College junior, Environmental Studies major, part-time barista
backstory: Jordan is passionate about sustainability and wants to work in the environmental sector, but has no idea how to break in. Their experience is limited to a part-time barista job and volunteering at an animal shelter. They've been scrolling job boards for weeks and feel overwhelmed — most postings seem to require 2+ years of experience even for 'entry-level' roles. They've never written a professional resume and have only had informal interviews. Their campus career center has long wait times, so they're looking for guidance on their own. They're motivated but don't know where to start.
career_goals: Land a summer internship in sustainability, environmental policy, or conservation
Build a resume that translates volunteer and coursework experience into professional skills
Understand what employers actually look for vs. what job postings say they require
challenges: No formal professional experience — only part-time service work and volunteering
Doesn't know how to tailor a resume for different types of roles
Intimidated by job postings that list requirements they don't fully meet
Unsure how to talk about coursework and volunteer work in a professional context
Nervous about interviews — has never done a formal one
existing_strengths: Genuinely curious and open to feedback
Consistent volunteer commitment at an animal shelter (2 years)
Good sense of humor and self-awareness
Willing to put in the work if someone shows them the path
current_situation: Jordan found three internship postings: one at a local conservation nonprofit, one at the city's sustainability office, and one at a solar energy startup. All three ask for a resume and cover letter. Jordan has a one-page resume that's mostly their barista job and a list of courses. They want help figuring out which roles are realistic, how to improve their resume, and how to actually apply.
what_success_looks_like: Jordan has a polished resume that highlights transferable skills, understands how to tailor applications for each role, feels prepared enough to submit all three applications, and knows what to expect in the interview process.
